SnowDistribution: Wet snowpack, but still good cover above 700 metres.  Some clear spells early morning, poor visibility during the afternoon.  Winds 20 -30 mph progress.
Comment: If more fresh snow than expected falls it might be enough to bump the hazard up to moderate level.
WeatherInfluences: After a mild night the freezing level will drop and be around 1000 metres during the day. There will be a few showers which will fall as snow above 900 metres. Winds will be from the South-West.
SnowStability: Above 1000 metres the existing snowpack will freeze, consolidate and be stable. Below this level it will continue to slowly thaw and will also be generally stable. Some fresh deposits of windslab are expected on North to East aspects above 1000 metres. However, these are expected to be shallow, reasonable well bonded and should be generally avoidable. The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: After a generally dry night and morning it was a damp afternoon with rain at all levels. Winds were from the South. The freezing level was around 1200 metres overnight rising above the summits during the day.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack consolidated slightly in the dry conditions overnight. However, significant thawing resumed during the day. Some wet snow instabilities exist on steep North to East aspects above 1000 metres. In places significant cornices overhang these aspects, and these remain prone to collapse.  The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

SnowDistribution: Thaw conditions.  Poor visibility particularly during rain in the afternoon.  Winds 30 -40 mph.
Comment: Sone variation in forecast snowfall amounts, however given the relatively high freezing level any new snow deposits should be reasonably well bonded.
WeatherInfluences: Occasional showers through the day are forecast to fall as snow above around 1000 metres. The freezing level will be above all summits overnight falling to be around 1000 metres during the day with strong South-Westerly winds.
SnowStability: The existing snowpack will continue to consolidate and stability will be good. The snow surface will begin to refreeze above around 1000 metres with a continued thaw at lower elevations. Any new wet snow deposits above 1000 metres are expected to be shallow and reasonably well bonded. The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: After dry spell overnight and through the morning rain at all levels developed from around midday. The freezing level rose from around 1100 metres to well above the highest summits with strong South-Westerly winds.
ObservedSnowStability: There is a general consolidating trend, however wet snow instabilities still exist on some steep slopes above around 950 metres. North-West through North to East aspects are most affected.  All remaining cornices are prone to collapse. The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

SnowDistribution: Wet snowpack in most locations. Some firmer patches holding on. Cloudy on the tops with some bright spells at lower elevations.  Winds 20-30 mph.
Comment:
Instability during a period of rain is expected in the evening and overnight. After which the potential for wet snow avalanches and cornice collapse will dissipate as freezing level falls, consolidating and firming up the snow pack.
Good timing of mountain activity will be an asset to capitalise on the best part of the day.
WeatherInfluences: A period of rain will occur at all levels in the evening. After a brief dry period rain is expected to transition to sleet and then snow above around 1000 metres. Winds will be very strong and from the West-South-West. The freezing level will be above the summits falling to around 1000 metres by dawn.
SnowStability: A period of instability will occur in the evening and overnight as the snow becomes saturated during a period of rain. There is the potential for cornice collapse and wet snow avalanches in steep locations on North-West, through North to East aspects above 850 metres. During the day a stabilising trend will become established, with snow becoming firm above 950 metres. Isolated, mostly avoidable, windslab deposits will start to develop on the same aspects. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Showers overnight with some snow on the highest summits. A dry morning was followed by persistent rain at all levels in the afternoon. Winds were mostly strong and from the South-West. The freezing level was above the highest summits at midday.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack is moist all levels, demonstrating instability with the onset of rising temperatures and rain. Debris was present from wet loose avalanche activity on North-West and North aspects at a height of 700 metres, along with evidence of cornice collapse. Snow loss has occurred overnight with most snow patches and ribbons being present above 600 metres. The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

SnowDistribution: A diminishing wet snowpack.  Poor visibility throughout the day above 600 metres. Winds 20 -30 mph.
Comment: The thaw continues this evening, with slightly cooler temperatures on Sunday, most likely giving a dusting of snow at summit level. Freezing level is set to rise well above the summits on Monday and into Tuesday, accompanied by rain, with significant snow loss likely during this period.
WeatherInfluences: Saturday evening will bring a few hours of rain to all levels. Throughout Sunday, there will be frequent showers of rain, with some sleet and snow on the highest tops.  Winds will be strong to very strong, South-Westerly. Overnight the freezing level will be well above the summits but will lower to around summit level by early Sunday morning, and remain there throughout the day.
SnowStability: Throughout Sunday, there will be some light fresh snow on the higher summits, where small accumulations may build on North through to East aspects above 850 metres but amounts are not forecast to be significant. The existing snowpack will continue to thaw and consolidate. The largest areas of which can be found above 800 metres, on West, through North and East to South aspects. The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: A very cloudy, low visibility day. Frequent light rain throughout. Winds were fresh to strong South-Westerly on the summits. The freezing level was around the tops in the morning, rising to above them in the afternoon.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack continues to thaw and consolidate at all levels and there has been some snow loss. Greatest accumulations remain above 800 metres, on West, through North and East to South aspects. Some icy, harder snow persists at higher elevations. The avalanche hazard is Low

SnowDistribution: Snowpack becoming soft and moist.  Sometimes cloudy on the tops with bright spells. Winds 30-40 mph at higher levels.
Comment: Large cornices observed in the Northern Corries.
WeatherInfluences: Rain at all levels during the first part of the night.  Light snowfall is expected from the middle of the night onwards above 1000 metres. West-South-Westerly winds will be gale force for the duration of the forecast period. Freezing level above the summits overnight before falling to 1000 metres from dawn onwards.
SnowStability: Unstable windslab will develop on wind sheltered North to East aspects above 1000 metres. Elsewhere the old snowpack will continue to consolidate; however on West through North to North-East aspects above 850 metres, localised instabilities will persist.  Areas most affected will be steep locations such as gullies, crag aprons and corrie back walls. Cornices will be unstable with potential to collapse triggering slopes below.  The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: A mild day with spells of heavy drizzle at all levels on fresh South-Easterly winds.  It was zero degrees at summit levels for the duration of the day.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack is thawing and consolidating at all altitudes; however snowpack instabilities were evident on steep West through North to North-East aspects mainly above 900 metres. Large unstable cornices overhang many of these aspects. Some instabilities are present below this altitude on steep and convex banks and burn lines. The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

SnowDistribution: Wet snow at all levels. Variable cloud base, lowering later in the afternoon. Strong winds first thing, eased by late morning.
Comment: Slopes above 1000m will become firm  as the wet snow refreezes.
WeatherInfluences: Light rain at all levels during the evening, followed by a dry night. Very light snowfall during the day above 950 metres.  Strong West-South-Westerly winds. 2000 metre freezing level during the evening, lowering to 1000 metres by daylight.
SnowStability: Rainfall and thaw conditions during the evening will increase the risk of cornice collapse. This will threaten moderately bonded slopes in wind sheltered locations on West through North to North-East aspects above 1000 metres. Later in the day, as the freezing level lowers, this snow will start to demonstrate good stability.  Below this altitude, the snow will be wet but generally stable. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: A mainly dry day with some light, intermittent precipitation during the afternoon, falling as sleet or wet snow only on the highest summits. Strong Westerly winds eased to moderate Southerlies. Freezing level around 1200 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: Thaw conditions have become established to summit level. Recent accumulations are consolidating with the majority of snow becoming generally stable but with wet surface layers. Moderately bonded snow persists in deeper accumulations in wind sheltered locations on West through North to North-East aspects above 1000 metres. Cornice collapse presents the greatest hazard, having the potential to trigger the slopes on the above mentioned aspects. The avalanche hazard is Moderate.
